title: pandanus indicus
intro: Pandanus indicus, also known as the screw pine, is a tropical plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Pandanus indicus include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and creams, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
